texto: She had read enough about teenagers to understand you couldn't confront them directly. You couldn't even agree with them. The best strategy was to feign indifference to whatever wrong direction they were headed in, then plop in little facts, like Alka-Seltzers, round innocuous comments, let those sink in, take slow, antidotal effect . . .
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Melissa Pritchard: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “She had read enough about teenagers to understand you couldn't confront them directly. You couldn…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, 1999, o-henry-memorial pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de o-henry-memorial e parenting.
